data.content_ref: inline:The proposed reduction sets k=1 to collapse the general threshold p_min(e,r,k) into a pairwise veto tradeoff, but this is mathematically invalid for the target artifact. The general theorem explicitly excludes k<5 because the majority is 'too thin for a robust filtering signal' (SC correctness relies on P[Bin(k,1-e)>k/2]). Setting k=1 destroys the majority-vote mechanism entirely (there is no 4/5 majority to erode), meaning the pairwise snapshot does not share the same mechanism. It is not a dimensional collapse of the threshold but a structural change to an entirely different decision process.
